title: Paramignya cuspidata
intro: Paramignya cuspidata is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Sri Lanka. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Paramignya cuspidata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are commonly consumed as herbal teas, tinctures, and capsules to harness its therapeutic benefits.

This family consists of around 160 genera and over 1,000 species, including various species of citrus and their relatives. The genus Paramignya is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Asia, and it is considered to be part of the larger Rutaceae family. The exact classification of Paramignya cuspidata may be further divided into more specific ranks within the botanical nomenclature system.
